content: ARCHITECTURE ANALYSIS (confidence: 0.88). DECISION 1 - SCHEMA: SEPARATE VIDEOS TABLE (not polymorphic Photos). CalendarEvents was given its own table in Phase 11 despite sharing columns with JournalSessions - that is the established pattern. Extending Photos with mediaType discriminator would require all 14 PhotoDao methods to add filtering. Recommended Videos table: videoId (PK UUID), sessionId (FK), messageId, localPath, thumbnailPath (non-nullable), cloudUrl, description, durationSeconds (INT), fileSizeBytes, syncStatus, createdAt, updatedAt. JournalMessages gets nullable videoId parallel to photoId. DECISION 2 - LIMITS: 60s cap enforced at recording layer (image_picker maxDuration), 100MB post-capture size cap. Sync must use streaming upload for files >20MB. DECISION 3 - SYNC BUCKET: Separate journal-videos bucket with independent limits. Add syncPendingVideos()/uploadSessionVideos() to SyncRepository, VideoDao injected via optional parameter. DECISION 4 - PLAYBACK: video_player (Flutter team). Follows ADR-0018 precedent of preferring built-ins. Build thin VideoPlayerWidget. DECISION 5 - METADATA: Use ffmpeg_kit_flutter min-GPL for metadata stripping (-map_metadata -1 -codec copy). Maintains privacy posture from ADR-0018/0019. ~8MB binary increase. Add load-bearing test. DECISION 6 - THUMBNAILS: video_thumbnail package, on-capture in Isolate.run(). Store at videos/{sessionId}/{videoId}_thumb.jpg. MISSING ADR: ADR-0021 required before implementation.

SECURITY ANALYSIS (confidence: 0.87). FINDING 1 (HIGH): GPS METADATA LEAKAGE. MP4 moov atom embeds GPS coordinates. Uploading raw video to Supabase bypasses ADR-0019 coordinate exclusion. Recommendation: Block video cloud sync until stripping is implemented. Local storage of unstripped video on-device is acceptable intermediate state. When stripping is implemented, use ffmpeg_kit_flutter with -map_metadata -1 -c:v copy -c:a copy. FINDING 2 (MEDIUM): PATH VALIDATION. Create separate VideoService.canonicalVideoPath and canonicalThumbnailPath with UUID regex validation. Consider tightening UUID regex from ^[a-f0-9-]+$ to full UUID format. FINDING 3 (MEDIUM): UPLOAD SIZE. Create separate journal-videos bucket with own file_size_limit and allowed_mime_types. Do not raise journal-photos limit. FINDING 4 (MEDIUM): MALFORMED VIDEO. Validate MP4 magic bytes (ftyp at bytes 4-8) before thumbnail extraction. Run in isolate for crash isolation. FINDING 5 (LOW): BUCKET SEPARATION. Separate journal-videos bucket preserves independent policy control per media type. FINDING 6 (LOW): UUID REGEX. Tighten to canonical UUID format for defense in depth. TRUST BOUNDARIES: Device Camera to App (strip metadata before downstream use), App Local Storage to Supabase Cloud (primary unresolved boundary for video GPS data).

PERFORMANCE ANALYSIS (confidence: 0.88). FINDING 1 (HIGH): UPLOAD OOM. Current uploadSessionPhotos uses readAsBytes() - 80MB video would allocate 80MB on Dart heap. Must use streaming File upload for videos. Reduce batch concurrency to 1 for video. FINDING 2 (HIGH): DURATION LIMITS. 60s hard cap keeps files at 50-80MB range. No current storage management beyond calculateTotalSize(). Warn user at 2GB total video, offer cleanup. FINDING 3 (HIGH): THUMBNAIL BINARY SIZE. video_thumbnail adds ~5MB vs ffmpeg_kit_flutter at ~30MB. Use video_thumbnail for thumbnails, not ffmpeg. Generate in Isolate.run() at 320x180 JPEG 70% quality (~15-25KB). FINDING 4 (MEDIUM): QUERY IMPACT. Separate Videos table keeps photo queries unmodified. watchAllPhotos() unchanged, add watchAllVideos(). FINDING 5 (MEDIUM): SEPARATE TABLE. Keeps each DAO focused, avoids branching in existing queries. FINDING 6 (MEDIUM): SYNC STRATEGY. WiFi-only, sequential, streaming upload. Use Supabase multipart for files >6MB. Add connectivity_plus check (already a dependency). Default 'Upload videos over mobile data' to off. FINDING 7 (MEDIUM): PLAYBACK MEMORY. Do NOT auto-initialize VideoPlayerController in chat list. Show static thumbnail with play overlay. Initialize controller only on tap. Dispose on modal close. video_player is sufficient. FINDING 8 (LOW): CONCURRENT MIC+CAMERA. Pause STT before video recording, resume after. QUANTITATIVE: Total APK addition ~7MB (video_player+video_thumbnail). Max video ~80MB at 60s. Thumbnail ~15-25KB.

INDEPENDENT ASSESSMENT (confidence: 0.82). HIDDEN ASSUMPTIONS CHALLENGED: (1) Video may not fit app soul - app is voice-first ambient journaling, video is screen-forward stationary. (2) 60s cap is cargo-culted from social media, no user research. (3) Polymorphic Photos table is NOT simpler per Principle 8 - maintenance complexity exceeds setup savings. (4) Video sync is categorically different from photo sync (30-60MB vs 200KB). (5) GPS metadata document-only contradicts established privacy posture. (6) No storage budget analysis. (7) Thumbnail may carry GPS from video container. PRE-MORTEM SCENARIOS: Storage exhaustion after 6 months (40 videos x 35MB = 1.4GB + 2GB LLM = 3.4GB). Polymorphic table causes gallery crash (getAllPhotos returns videos, Image.file crashes on .mp4). Silent sync failure loses videos permanently. video_player Gradle conflict (no spike validation). Video capture during voice mode causes audio focus conflict. ALTERNATIVES: (A) Video as opaque file attachment - open in native player, eliminates video_player and video_thumbnail dependencies. (B) Separate Videos table from day one. (C) Defer video entirely, extend voice notes instead - voice notes are 1-2MB for 60s vs 30-60MB, compatible with voice-first model, record package already in dependencies. CONSENSUS RISK: Plan pre-selects answers framed as questions. Specialists may validate rather than challenge. STRONG RECOMMENDATION: Run a dependency spike (add video_player+video_thumbnail, build Android, test on Galaxy S21 Ultra) before any feature code, per ADR-0015 spike precedent.

ROUND 2: Dispute is narrower than it appears. Min-GPL variant is ~8-12MB, not 30MB. Middle path: Ship with local-only video at launch (security-specialist blocking position), with ffmpeg_kit_flutter min-GPL already wired but cloud sync feature-flagged off. (1) ffmpeg strips metadata before local storage - binary cost paid at launch, matches ADR-0018 strip-at-ingest model. (2) Cloud sync disabled via feature flag, identical to ADR-0019 opt-in location. (3) Unit test asserts upload path unreachable for video until flag enabled. Once ffmpeg stripping validated in production, enabling sync is a one-line flag change. video_thumbnail recommendation for thumbnails is orthogonal and correct.

ROUND 2: APPROVE phased approach with one enforcement condition. Phase 1 (local-only) is clean - no sync means no GPS exfiltration attack surface. Phase 2 must gate sync activation on stripping being in place. ADR must encode this as a hard dependency: upload code path must be unreachable unless stripMetadata() completed successfully. Same pattern ADR-0018 used for photos. 8MB (min-GPL) binary cost is acceptable for user privacy. -map_metadata -1 -codec copy avoids re-encoding (no quality loss). Approve contingent on ADR explicitly stating sync enablement is blocked until stripping is wired as hard gate.

SYNTHESIS — VIDEO CAPTURE ARCHITECTURE (4 specialists, 2 rounds, dialectic mode)

UNANIMOUS AGREEMENT on 5 of 6 decisions. One resolved tension on metadata stripping.

DECISION 1 - SCHEMA: SEPARATE VIDEOS TABLE. All 4 specialists rejected the polymorphic Photos table approach. Architecture-consultant cited CalendarEvents precedent (Phase 11). Independent-perspective identified regression risk (getAllPhotos returning videos crashes Image.file). Performance-analyst confirmed no performance benefit to shared table. Create Videos table with dedicated VideoDao following ADR-0007 pattern.

DECISION 2 - LIMITS: 60s hard cap at recording layer (image_picker maxDuration). Performance-analyst provided quantitative backing: 50-80MB per video at 1080p. Independent-perspective noted 60s is arbitrary but acceptable as starting point. Add storage budget warning at 2GB total video.

DECISION 3 - SYNC BUCKET: Separate journal-videos bucket. Architecture-consultant, security-specialist, and performance-analyst aligned. Different size envelopes (10MB photos vs 150+MB videos) need independent bucket-level limits and MIME policies. Follows CalendarEvents precedent.

DECISION 4 - PLAYBACK: video_player (Flutter team). Architecture-consultant cited ADR-0018 precedent of preferring built-ins. Performance-analyst recommended static thumbnail with play overlay (no auto-init controllers in list). Independent-perspective offered alternative of native player via Intent (deferred as MVP-2 option).

DECISION 5 - METADATA: PHASED APPROACH (key resolved tension). Round 1: Architecture-consultant recommended ffmpeg_kit_flutter min-GPL (~8MB) for stripping. Security-specialist recommended blocking sync until stripping exists. Performance-analyst warned about binary size. Round 2 convergence: Ship local-only video at launch with ffmpeg_kit_flutter min-GPL wired for on-device stripping. Cloud sync feature-flagged off until stripping validated. ADR must encode sync-gate as hard dependency (upload unreachable without prior stripMetadata). Load-bearing test enforces this.

DECISION 6 - THUMBNAILS: video_thumbnail package (~5MB). On-capture generation in Isolate.run(). 320x180 JPEG at 70% quality. Store at videos/{sessionId}/{videoId}_thumb.jpg. Orthogonal to metadata stripping.

INDEPENDENT-PERSPECTIVE CHALLENGES ADDRESSED:
- Voice notes alternative: Valid but different feature. Video serves 'visual moment capture' use case that voice notes cannot. Proceed with video but document scope.
- Dependency spike: ADOPTED. Must validate video_player + video_thumbnail + ffmpeg_kit_flutter Android build on Galaxy S21 Ultra before feature implementation.
- Audio focus conflict: Pause STT before video recording, resume after. Explicitly defined.
- Storage budget: Warn at 2GB, offer cleanup. calculateTotalSize() extended to videos.

TOTAL APK IMPACT: ~15MB (video_player ~2MB + video_thumbnail ~5MB + ffmpeg_kit_flutter min-GPL ~8MB).

ADR-0021 REQUIRED before implementation.

DECISIONS REACHED (6/6):

1. SCHEMA: Separate Videos table + VideoDao (not polymorphic Photos). Schema v6 migration.
2. LIMITS: 60s recording cap, 100MB post-capture cap, 2GB storage budget warning.
3. SYNC BUCKET: New journal-videos Supabase Storage bucket (separate from journal-photos).
4. PLAYBACK: video_player package. Static thumbnails in list, full player on tap.
5. METADATA: ffmpeg_kit_flutter min-GPL for on-device stripping. Cloud sync feature-flagged off at launch. Sync-gate enforced by load-bearing test.
6. THUMBNAILS: video_thumbnail package. On-capture in Isolate.run(). 320x180 JPEG 70%.

PREREQUISITES:
- ADR-0021 must be written before implementation
- Dependency spike must validate Android build with video_player + video_thumbnail + ffmpeg_kit_flutter on Galaxy S21 Ultra
- Audio focus interaction with STT must be explicitly handled (pause STT during video recording)
